facts.AK: Alaska is larger than Texas, California, and Montana combined.
facts.AL: Alabama was the first US state to declare Christmas a legal holiday in 1836.
facts.AZ: Arizona averages over 300 sunny days per year.
facts.CA: California has the largest state economy in the US.
facts.CO: Colorado has 53 peaks above 14,000 ft, more than any other state.
facts.CT: Connecticut is home to the US Navys oldest continuously operating submarine base.
facts.DC: Washington DC has more free museums per capita than anywhere in the US.
facts.DE: Delaware was the first state to ratify the US Constitution in 1787.
facts.FL: Florida has over 1,350 miles of coastline, the longest of the lower 48 states.
facts.GA: Georgia is the largest state east of the Mississippi River.
facts.GU: Guam was the first Pacific island liberated in WWII.
facts.HI: Hawaii is the only US state that grows coffee commercially.
facts.IA: Iowa produces about 10% of the entire US food supply.
facts.ID: Idaho grows about one-third of all US potatoes.
facts.IL: Chicago is home to the first skyscraper ever built, completed in 1885.
facts.IN: Indiana hosts the Indianapolis 500, one of the oldest motorsport races.
facts.KS: Kansas sits at the geographic center of the contiguous United States.
facts.KY: The Kentucky Derby has run every May since 1875.
facts.LA: New Orleans operates the oldest continuously running street railway in the world.
facts.MA: Massachusetts opened the first public school in America in 1635.
facts.MD: The US Naval Academy founded in 1845 is located in Annapolis, Maryland.
facts.ME: Maine produces 99% of all blueberries commercially harvested in the US.
facts.MI: Michigan borders four of the five Great Lakes.
facts.MN: Minnesota is the source of the Mississippi River and has over 10,000 lakes.
facts.MO: The Gateway Arch in St. Louis is the tallest US monument at 630 feet.
facts.MS: Mississippi is the birthplace of the Blues.
facts.MT: Montana has more cattle than people.
facts.NC: Kitty Hawk NC is where the Wright Brothers made the first powered flight in 1903.
facts.ND: North Dakota is one of Americas leading energy-producing states.
facts.NE: Nebraska has more miles of river than any other US state.
facts.NH: New Hampshires motto Live Free or Die reflects its fierce independent spirit.
facts.NJ: New Jersey has the highest population density of any US state.
facts.NM: Santa Fe NM is the oldest state capital in the US, founded in 1610.
facts.NV: Nevada receives about 300 sunny days per year.
facts.NY: New York City is home to over 800 spoken languages.
facts.OH: Ohio has produced more US Presidents than any other state.
facts.OK: Oklahoma has more man-made lakes than any other US state.
facts.OR: Crater Lake Oregon is the deepest lake in the US at 1,943 feet.
facts.PA: Philadelphia is the birthplace of American democracy.
facts.PR: San Juan Puerto Rico is one of the oldest European-founded cities in the Americas.
facts.RI: Rhode Island is the smallest US state yet has 400 miles of coastline.
facts.SC: Fort Sumter SC was the site of the first shots of the Civil War.
facts.SD: Mount Rushmore and the Badlands are among Americas most iconic landmarks.
facts.TN: Tennessee is the birthplace of country music.
facts.TX: Texas would rank as the 40th largest country if it were independent.
facts.UT: Utahs five national parks are among the most stunning in the world.
facts.VA: Virginia is the birthplace of more US Presidents than any other state.
facts.VT: Vermont produces more maple syrup per capita than any other US state.
facts.WA: Washington State grows more apples than any other state.
facts.WI: Wisconsin produces more cheese than any other US state.
facts.WV: West Virginia has one of the highest per-capita military service rates.
facts.WY: Wyoming was the first US territory to grant women the right to vote in 1869.
facts.Japan: Japan is home to the worlds oldest company, Kongo Gumi, founded in 578 AD.
facts.Germany: Germany has over 1,500 beer varieties and 1,300 active breweries.
facts.Italy: Italy has more UNESCO World Heritage Sites than any other country.
facts.Bahrain: Bahrain was the first Gulf state to discover oil, in 1932.
facts.Greece: Greece has the longest coastline in Europe at over 8,500 miles.
facts.Turkey: Istanbul is the only city in the world on two continents.
facts.UK: The Royal Air Force founded in 1918 is one of the worlds oldest air forces.
facts.S. Korea: South Korea ranks first in the world for average internet speed.
facts.Belgium: Belgium has more Michelin-starred restaurants per capita than France.
facts.Djibouti: Djibouti hosts one of the worlds most strategically important military bases.
